legal.summary: Committee members must disclose conflicts of interest and recuse themselves from affected deliberations.

requirements.requirement: Any Committee member with a direct or indirect interest in a matter under discussion must disclose the fact and nature of the interest to the Committee
requirements.rationale: Ensures governance integrity of NCII designation and policy decisions at the highest level

requirements.requirement: Disclosed interests must be recorded in the minutes of the Committee meeting
requirements.rationale: Creates auditable record of governance compliance

technical.summary: Technical controls to support disclosure-of-interest governance and auditability.

requirements.requirement: Implement third-party security assessment framework with standardized questionnaires
requirements.rationale: Systematic assessment ensures consistent evaluation of external party interests and security posture

requirements.requirement: Deploy continuous monitoring of third-party network connections and data flows
requirements.rationale: Real-time visibility into external party access supports governance oversight and conflict detection

requirements.requirement: Maintain automated vendor risk scoring system with real-time threat intelligence feeds
requirements.rationale: Automated scoring provides objective, data-driven input for disclosure and recusal decisions

governance.summary: Conflict-of-interest management framework for the national cyber security governance body.

requirements.requirement: Member must recuse from all deliberation and decision-making on the disclosed matter after making the disclosure
requirements.rationale: Prevents conflicted decision-making on NCII designations and sector determinations

legal.summary: CE directives are binding instruments that NCII entities and sector leads must comply with.

requirements.requirement: Monitor, identify and maintain a register of all CE directives issued under the Act, ensuring awareness of each directive's requirements and effective date
requirements.rationale: CE directives form a critical layer of the regulatory framework — non-compliance with any directive constitutes an offence under s18

requirements.requirement: Ensure organizational compliance with each directive within specified implementation timelines
requirements.rationale: Directives published in Gazette have binding legal force on all NCII entities

technical.summary: Technical compliance with the specific requirements of each CE directive.

requirements.requirement: Implement technical controls and configurations specified in each applicable CE directive
requirements.rationale: Directives may mandate specific technical measures such as incident reporting formats, risk assessment methodologies, or security configurations

governance.summary: Governance framework for tracking and implementing CE directives.

requirements.requirement: Establish a directive tracking and compliance monitoring process, including gap analysis for each new directive and remediation planning
requirements.rationale: Systematic approach needed given approximately 10 directives already published with more expected

s17.title: Designation of NCII entity and NCII sector lead

legal.summary: Gateway provision for all Part IV obligations. Designation as an NCII entity triggers the full compliance duty catalogue.

requirements.requirement: Identify whether the organization owns or operates infrastructure that could qualify as NCII and proactively assess designation risk
requirements.rationale: Once designated, the entity bears immediate obligations under ss18-26 with severe penalties for non-compliance

requirements.requirement: Upon receiving a designation notice, verify the scope of designated NCII assets and understand the full compliance obligations triggered
requirements.rationale: Designation scope determines which systems are subject to risk assessment, audit, and incident notification requirements

requirements.requirement: If aggrieved by designation, file an appeal to the Minister within 30 days of notification
requirements.rationale: Strict 30-day time limit for appeals; failure to appeal within this period results in designation becoming final

technical.summary: Asset identification and scoping for NCII designation.

requirements.requirement: Maintain a comprehensive asset inventory identifying all systems that may qualify as NCII, including their criticality assessment and interdependencies
requirements.rationale: CE considers the nature, extent, and consequences of compromise — organizations need to understand their own NCII landscape

requirements.requirement: Map system dependencies and identify single points of failure for designated NCII assets
requirements.rationale: Understanding the blast radius of infrastructure compromise is essential for both designation assessment and subsequent compliance

governance.summary: Board-level awareness and governance for NCII designation and its implications.

requirements.requirement: Brief the Board on NCII designation implications including full compliance obligations, penalty exposure (up to RM500,000/10 years), and director liability under s52
requirements.rationale: Board must understand that designation creates personal liability for directors and officers

requirements.requirement: Appoint a designated NCII compliance officer and establish clear accountability for Act compliance
requirements.rationale: Cross-functional compliance program requires dedicated ownership given breadth of obligations

legal.summary: Comprehensive duty catalogue for NCII entities. Breach of any duty is a criminal offence (RM500,000/10 years).

requirements.requirement: Implement and maintain cyber security measures to protect NCII, ensuring measures are current, effective, and proportionate to the risk profile
requirements.rationale: Overarching obligation encompassing all technical and organizational measures for NCII protection

requirements.requirement: Comply with all applicable standards, codes of practice, and directives issued under the Act
requirements.rationale: s18(1)(b) creates a legal obligation to comply with subsidiary instruments, making non-compliance with any standard/CoP/directive a criminal offence

requirements.requirement: Report cyber security incidents to the CE in accordance with s26 notification requirements
requirements.rationale: s18(1)(c) duty to report, read with s26 6-hour timeline for significant incidents

requirements.requirement: Conduct or cause to be conducted cyber security risk assessments per s21 requirements
requirements.rationale: s18(1)(d) duty read with s21 for prescribed frequency and approved assessor requirement

requirements.requirement: Conduct or cause to be conducted cyber security audits per s23 requirements
requirements.rationale: s18(1)(e) duty read with s23 for prescribed frequency and approved auditor requirement

requirements.requirement: Provide information as required by the CE or NCII sector lead under s20
requirements.rationale: s18(1)(f) duty to cooperate with regulatory information requests

technical.summary: Technical implementation of NCII protection measures across the full cyber security lifecycle.

requirements.requirement: Deploy and maintain defence-in-depth security architecture for all designated NCII assets, including network segmentation, access controls, encryption, and monitoring
requirements.rationale: Duty to implement and maintain cyber security measures requires a comprehensive technical security program

requirements.requirement: Implement 24/7 security monitoring capability for designated NCII, with alerting and escalation for potential incidents
requirements.rationale: 6-hour incident notification window requires real-time detection capability

requirements.requirement: Maintain current patch management and vulnerability remediation processes for NCII assets
requirements.rationale: Known vulnerabilities in NCII assets could constitute failure to maintain cyber security measures

governance.summary: Governance framework for NCII entity duties and compliance program management.

requirements.requirement: Establish a comprehensive NCII compliance program with clear policies, procedures, roles and responsibilities covering all s18(1) duties
requirements.rationale: Multiple concurrent obligations require an integrated compliance management approach

requirements.requirement: Report quarterly to the Board on NCII compliance status, covering risk assessment results, audit findings, incident history, and remediation progress
requirements.rationale: Director liability under s52 requires boards to demonstrate active oversight of NCII compliance

legal.summary: Sector leads must coordinate, monitor, and support NCII entities within their sector.

requirements.requirement: Coordinate and monitor the cyber security posture of all NCII in the designated sector
requirements.rationale: Sector leads are the regulatory intermediary between NACSA and individual NCII entities

requirements.requirement: Develop and recommend sector-specific codes of practice for CE approval under s22
requirements.rationale: CoPs tailor the Act's general requirements to sector-specific contexts and risk profiles

requirements.requirement: Report to the CE on the state of cyber security in the sector
requirements.rationale: Feeds into national-level situational awareness and policy development

technical.summary: Technical coordination and advisory functions of sector leads.

requirements.requirement: Establish sector-level threat intelligence sharing mechanism and coordinate sectoral incident response
requirements.rationale: Sector leads must be technically capable of monitoring and coordinating cyber security across their sector

governance.summary: Sector-level governance framework for NCII oversight.

requirements.requirement: Establish a sector-level NCII governance forum for regular engagement with NCII entities, including compliance support and best-practice sharing
requirements.rationale: s19(1)(c) duty to advise NCII entities and s19(1)(e) duty to assist compliance require structured engagement

legal.summary: NCII entities must provide information and documents when requested by the CE, with criminal penalties for non-compliance or providing false information.

requirements.requirement: Respond to CE information requests within the specified period, providing complete and accurate information about NCII assets, security posture, and operations
requirements.rationale: Non-compliance carries RM200,000/3 years; false information carries RM300,000/5 years

requirements.requirement: Establish internal processes to collect and verify NCII information rapidly, ensuring responses can meet CE-specified timelines
requirements.rationale: Information requests may have tight deadlines; organizations need ready access to asset inventories, configuration data, and security documentation

technical.summary: Technical capability to produce accurate NCII information on demand.

requirements.requirement: Maintain up-to-date NCII asset register with system configurations, network diagrams, data flow maps, and security control inventory
requirements.rationale: CE information requests may require detailed technical documentation about NCII assets

governance.summary: Governance processes for handling regulatory information requests.

requirements.requirement: Establish a regulatory request handling procedure with defined roles, escalation paths, and quality assurance checks to ensure accuracy of responses
requirements.rationale: Criminal liability for false or misleading information requires robust verification before submission

legal.summary: Mandatory periodic and on-demand risk assessments by approved persons, with results submitted to CE and sector lead.

requirements.requirement: Conduct cyber security risk assessments at intervals not exceeding the prescribed period (typically annually) for all designated NCII
requirements.rationale: Non-compliance carries RM500,000/10 years — one of the highest penalty tiers under the Act

requirements.requirement: Conduct additional risk assessments within the period specified when directed by the CE
requirements.rationale: CE may direct ad-hoc assessments in response to emerging threats, sector-wide incidents, or compliance concerns

requirements.requirement: Engage only CE-approved persons to conduct the risk assessment
requirements.rationale: Assessment quality assurance — approved persons are typically licensed cyber security service providers under Part V

requirements.requirement: Submit risk assessment results to the CE and the relevant NCII sector lead within prescribed timeframes
requirements.rationale: Results inform NACSA's national NCII risk picture and the sector lead's oversight function

technical.summary: Technical execution of cyber security risk assessment methodology.

requirements.requirement: Apply the risk assessment methodology specified in the Cyber Security (Risk Assessment and Audit) Regulations 2024, covering threat identification, vulnerability assessment, impact analysis, and risk evaluation for all NCII assets
requirements.rationale: Methodology must align with CE-prescribed standards to ensure consistency across the national NCII landscape

requirements.requirement: Include assessment of supply chain risks, third-party dependencies, and interconnected infrastructure in the NCII risk assessment scope
requirements.rationale: NCII risk assessments must consider the full attack surface, not just internal systems

requirements.requirement: Develop and implement a risk treatment plan addressing all identified risks, with clear timelines, resource allocation, and accountable owners for each treatment action
requirements.rationale: Risk assessment is only valuable if findings are acted upon; treatment plan demonstrates due diligence

governance.summary: Governance framework for risk assessment program management.

requirements.requirement: Present risk assessment results and treatment plan to the Board, obtaining formal approval and resource commitment for remediation activities
requirements.rationale: Director liability (s52) requires board-level engagement with NCII risk findings

requirements.requirement: Track risk treatment plan implementation progress and report on residual risk status to the CE/sector lead as required
requirements.rationale: Ongoing accountability for risk remediation beyond the assessment itself

legal.summary: NCII entities must comply with CE-approved codes of practice applicable to their sector.

requirements.requirement: Identify all codes of practice approved under s22 that apply to the organization's NCII sector and ensure full compliance
requirements.rationale: Non-compliance with CoPs is a criminal offence (RM200,000/3 years)

requirements.requirement: Monitor for CE variations, revocations, or replacements of applicable codes of practice and implement changes within specified timeframes
requirements.rationale: CoPs may be updated as threats evolve; entities must maintain compliance with current versions

technical.summary: Implementation of sector-specific CoP technical requirements.

requirements.requirement: Implement the technical standards, security controls, and best practices specified in the applicable sector CoP
requirements.rationale: CoPs translate the Act's general cyber security duty into sector-specific, actionable technical requirements

governance.summary: Governance for CoP compliance tracking.

requirements.requirement: Conduct periodic gap analysis between current practices and CoP requirements, maintaining a compliance status register
requirements.rationale: Structured tracking ensures sustained compliance and identifies gaps proactively before audit

legal.summary: Mandatory periodic and on-demand cyber security audits by approved persons, with results submitted to CE and sector lead.

requirements.requirement: Conduct cyber security audits at intervals not exceeding the prescribed period (typically annually) for all designated NCII
requirements.rationale: Non-compliance carries RM500,000/10 years — same penalty tier as risk assessment failure

requirements.requirement: Conduct additional audits within the period specified when directed by the CE
requirements.rationale: CE may direct audits in response to incidents, risk assessment findings, or compliance concerns

requirements.requirement: Engage only CE-approved persons to conduct the audit
requirements.rationale: Audit quality assurance — approved auditors must hold valid licences under Part V

requirements.requirement: Submit audit results to the CE and the relevant NCII sector lead within prescribed timeframes
requirements.rationale: Audit results inform regulatory oversight and sectoral compliance monitoring

technical.summary: Technical scope and execution of cyber security audit.

requirements.requirement: Audit scope must cover compliance with the Act, regulations, directives, applicable codes of practice, and standards determined by the CE under s24
requirements.rationale: Comprehensive audit scope ensures the full regulatory framework is assessed, not just technical controls

requirements.requirement: Include technical testing (vulnerability assessment, penetration testing, configuration review) as part of the audit, proportionate to the NCII risk profile
requirements.rationale: Audit must go beyond document review to verify actual security posture of NCII assets

governance.summary: Governance for audit program management and remediation tracking.

requirements.requirement: Present audit findings to the Board and develop a remediation plan with clear timelines for addressing each finding
requirements.rationale: Board oversight of audit findings demonstrates due diligence; unaddressed findings create ongoing compliance risk

requirements.requirement: Track remediation progress for all audit findings and ensure closure within agreed timelines, with escalation for overdue items
requirements.rationale: Demonstrating remediation effort is critical for subsequent audit cycles and regulatory engagement

legal.summary: NCII entities must comply with CE-determined cyber security standards within specified timeframes.

requirements.requirement: Comply with all cyber security standards determined by the CE under s24(1) within the implementation period specified
requirements.rationale: Non-compliance is a criminal offence (RM200,000/3 years)

technical.summary: Technical implementation of CE-determined standards.

requirements.requirement: Conduct gap analysis between current security posture and each applicable CE-determined standard, implementing required controls within the specified compliance deadline
requirements.rationale: Standards may reference international frameworks (ISO 27001, NIST CSF) or prescribe specific technical configurations

governance.summary: Standards compliance management and monitoring.

requirements.requirement: Maintain a standards compliance register tracking the organization's status against each applicable CE-determined standard
requirements.rationale: Multiple standards may apply; structured tracking ensures nothing falls through the cracks

s25.title: Duty to provide information on threats

legal.summary: NCII entities must share threat information that could affect other NCII with the CE as soon as practicable.

requirements.requirement: Report to the CE as soon as practicable any information relating to a cyber security threat or incident that may affect other NCII beyond the entity's own infrastructure
requirements.rationale: Enables cross-sector early warning and coordinated response; non-compliance carries RM200,000/3 years

technical.summary: Technical capability for threat intelligence sharing.

requirements.requirement: Establish processes and channels to identify, assess and rapidly share threat intelligence relevant to other NCII with the CE, including indicators of compromise, attack patterns, and vulnerability disclosures
requirements.rationale: Sharing must be timely ('as soon as practicable') — requires automation and pre-established communication channels

governance.summary: Governance for threat intelligence sharing decisions.

requirements.requirement: Define criteria and escalation procedures for determining when threat information may affect other NCII and triggers the s25 sharing obligation
requirements.rationale: Employees need clear guidance on what constitutes a reportable threat to avoid both under-reporting and over-reporting

legal.summary: NCII entities must notify the CE of cyber security incidents within 6 hours (significant impact) or other prescribed periods. Heaviest penalties in Part IV.

requirements.requirement: Notify the CE within 6 hours of becoming aware of a cyber security incident that has a significant impact on NCII
requirements.rationale: Most time-critical obligation under the Act; non-compliance carries RM500,000/10 years plus RM5,000/day continuing offence

requirements.requirement: For incidents without significant impact, notify the CE within the period prescribed by regulations
requirements.rationale: Non-significant incidents still require notification, though with a longer timeline

requirements.requirement: Include all prescribed information in the incident notification, ensuring accuracy and completeness
requirements.rationale: False or misleading notification is a separate offence (RM300,000/5 years under s26(4))

technical.summary: Technical incident detection and notification capability.

requirements.requirement: Deploy SIEM, IDS/IPS, EDR, and other detection technologies capable of identifying cyber security incidents affecting NCII in near-real-time
requirements.rationale: 6-hour clock starts at 'becoming aware' — organizations must have capability to detect incidents rapidly

requirements.requirement: Establish pre-configured incident notification templates and secure communication channels to NACSA, enabling sub-6-hour notification
requirements.rationale: Notification templates reduce response time and ensure prescribed information fields are not missed during high-pressure incident response

requirements.requirement: Implement incident classification framework with clear criteria for determining 'significant impact' triggering the 6-hour notification window
requirements.rationale: Rapid triage is essential — the clock starts at awareness, not at classification

governance.summary: Incident response governance and readiness.

requirements.requirement: Maintain a documented, tested incident response plan (IRP) with clear roles, escalation procedures, and notification workflows aligned with the 6-hour timeline
requirements.rationale: IRP must be operationally ready — not just documented — with regular tabletop exercises and drills

requirements.requirement: Ensure 24/7 availability of personnel authorized to approve and send incident notifications to the CE
requirements.rationale: 6-hour notification window applies regardless of time of day; requires on-call arrangements and delegated authority

requirements.requirement: Conduct post-incident reviews for all notified incidents, documenting lessons learned and implementing improvements
requirements.rationale: Continuous improvement of incident response capability reduces future risk and demonstrates mature governance

s28.title: Licensing of cyber security service providers

legal.summary: Mandatory licensing for all persons providing prescribed cyber security services.

requirements.requirement: Obtain a valid licence from the CE before providing any cyber security service as defined in s2
requirements.rationale: Unlicensed provision is a criminal offence (RM500,000/10 years)

requirements.requirement: Comply with all conditions imposed on the licence by the CE
requirements.rationale: Licence conditions may include qualifications, staffing, insurance, and operational requirements

technical.summary: Technical competency requirements for licensed service providers.

requirements.requirement: Maintain the technical competencies, certifications, tools, and infrastructure required by licence conditions for each licensed service category
requirements.rationale: Licensed services include SOC monitoring, penetration testing, and incident response — each requiring specific technical capabilities

governance.summary: Governance for licensing compliance by service providers.

requirements.requirement: For NCII entities: verify that all engaged cyber security service providers hold valid licences under Part V before engagement and throughout the service period
requirements.rationale: Engaging unlicensed providers may constitute failure to comply with s18 duties

legal.summary: Licensed cyber security service providers must maintain professional standards, confidentiality, and records.

requirements.requirement: Maintain standards of competence, integrity and professionalism in the provision of all cyber security services
requirements.rationale: Professional standards obligation applies to all licensed activities

requirements.requirement: Maintain strict confidentiality of all information obtained in the course of providing cyber security services
requirements.rationale: Service providers have deep access to client NCII; unauthorized disclosure could compromise national security

requirements.requirement: Keep and maintain comprehensive records of all cyber security services provided, including scope, findings, and recommendations
requirements.rationale: Records support regulatory oversight and audit trail for services rendered to NCII entities

technical.summary: Technical obligations for licensed service providers.

requirements.requirement: Implement information security controls to protect client information, including encryption, access controls, and secure data handling procedures
requirements.rationale: Confidentiality obligation requires technical protection of sensitive NCII data obtained during service delivery

governance.summary: Governance for licensee professional obligations.

requirements.requirement: Implement a quality management system covering staff competency, service delivery standards, record keeping, and compliance with CE directives and guidelines
requirements.rationale: Systematic approach to meeting the broad professional obligations under s32

legal.summary: Entities must not obstruct authorized officers exercising enforcement powers.

requirements.requirement: Cooperate fully with authorized officers conducting investigations, inspections, or enforcement activities under Part VI, providing requested access, documents, and information
requirements.rationale: Obstruction carries RM300,000/5 years; includes refusing access, hindering officers, refusing to produce documents, or refusing to answer questions

technical.summary: Technical cooperation requirements during enforcement.

requirements.requirement: Be prepared to provide passwords, encryption keys, decryption codes, and any other means required for authorized officers to access computerized data during searches (per s37)
requirements.rationale: Failure to provide technical access constitutes obstruction under s44(c)

governance.summary: Governance for handling enforcement interactions.

requirements.requirement: Establish a documented procedure for interacting with NACSA authorized officers during inspections and investigations, including designated points of contact and escalation to legal counsel
requirements.rationale: Structured response prevents accidental obstruction while protecting the organization's rights

legal.summary: Information acquired under the Act must be kept confidential; unauthorized disclosure is a criminal offence.

requirements.requirement: Ensure all persons performing functions under the Act maintain strict confidentiality of information acquired in the course of those functions
requirements.rationale: Breach of secrecy carries RM500,000/10 years — one of the highest penalties under the Act

requirements.requirement: Only disclose Act-related information for permitted purposes: purposes of the Act, civil/criminal proceedings, or with written CE consent
requirements.rationale: Three narrow exceptions define the boundaries of permitted disclosure

technical.summary: Technical controls for protecting Act-related information.

requirements.requirement: Implement information classification and handling procedures for Act-related data (incident reports, risk assessments, audit results, threat intelligence), with access restricted to authorized personnel
requirements.rationale: Technical controls must enforce the secrecy obligation across all systems and channels

governance.summary: Governance framework for secrecy obligations.

requirements.requirement: Include Act secrecy obligations in staff training, employment contracts, and NDA arrangements for all personnel with access to Act-related information
requirements.rationale: Staff awareness and contractual commitments are the first line of defence against unauthorized disclosure

legal.summary: Directors and officers face personal criminal liability for corporate offences unless they prove due diligence. Corporate fines may be multiplied by 10x.

requirements.requirement: Directors, CEO, COO, managers and officers must be able to demonstrate that they exercised all due diligence to prevent the commission of any offence by the body corporate
requirements.rationale: Reverse burden of proof — officers are deemed to have committed the offence unless they can prove due diligence and lack of knowledge/consent/connivance

requirements.requirement: Maintain evidence of board-level engagement with NCII compliance, including meeting minutes, compliance reports reviewed, decisions made, and resources allocated
requirements.rationale: Due diligence defence requires demonstrable evidence of active governance, not passive awareness

technical.summary: Technical controls enabling board-level visibility and due diligence evidence for corporate officer liability.

requirements.requirement: Implement board-level cybersecurity dashboard with real-time compliance metrics
requirements.rationale: Directors need continuous visibility into NCII compliance posture to demonstrate active oversight and due diligence

requirements.requirement: Deploy automated compliance monitoring for all NCII systems with alerting
requirements.rationale: Automated monitoring provides evidence that the body corporate maintained continuous compliance oversight, supporting due diligence defence

requirements.requirement: Maintain audit trail of all corporate officer access to NCII management systems
requirements.rationale: Demonstrates that officers actively engaged with compliance systems, supporting their due diligence defence under s52

governance.summary: Board governance framework to support due diligence defence.

requirements.requirement: Establish a Board Cyber Security Committee or assign NCII compliance oversight to an existing board committee with regular reporting cadence
requirements.rationale: Structured board oversight is the foundation of a credible due diligence defence under s52

requirements.requirement: Ensure adequate resources (budget, staffing, tools) are allocated for NCII compliance, with board-approved budget for cyber security risk treatment
requirements.rationale: Demonstrating resource allocation is key evidence of due diligence by directors

legal.summary: The Agency provides secretariat and administrative support to the Committee.

requirements.requirement: Ensure the entity cooperates with and responds to communications from the Agency in its capacity as secretariat to the Committee
requirements.rationale: The Agency acts as the conduit for Committee decisions and requirements; unresponsive entities risk non-compliance

technical.summary: Technical support for Agency/Committee communication channels.

requirements.requirement: Maintain secure and reliable communication channels with the Agency for receiving Committee-originated directives, notices, and requests
requirements.rationale: Ensures timely receipt of Committee decisions transmitted through the Agency secretariat

governance.summary: Governance for handling Committee communications via the Agency.

requirements.requirement: Assign a designated point of contact for receiving and actioning communications from the Agency in its secretariat capacity
requirements.rationale: Prevents Committee-originated communications from being misdirected or delayed

legal.summary: The Chief Executive may share information received under Part IV with sector leads, NCII entities, government entities, and foreign countries or international organizations for NCII protection.

requirements.requirement: Accept and act upon threat intelligence or other information shared by the Chief Executive under s27 for NCII protection purposes
requirements.rationale: Information sharing is a key mechanism for cross-sector and international NCII protection coordination

requirements.requirement: Use information shared by the Chief Executive only for the purpose of protecting the national critical information infrastructure, as required by s27(2)
requirements.rationale: Shared information is purpose-limited; misuse may breach the Act and undermine trust in the sharing framework

technical.summary: Technical controls for receiving and protecting shared threat intelligence.

requirements.requirement: Implement secure channels for receiving information shared by the Chief Executive, with access controls limiting use to authorised NCII protection personnel
requirements.rationale: Shared intelligence must be protected from unauthorised access and limited to NCII protection purposes

governance.summary: Governance for managing shared information from the Chief Executive.

requirements.requirement: Establish a process for receiving, classifying, disseminating, and actioning information shared by the Chief Executive, with audit trail and purpose-limitation controls
requirements.rationale: Structured handling ensures shared intelligence is used effectively while complying with purpose-limitation requirements

legal.summary: Licences are valid for 3 years and must be renewed by application not less than 30 days before expiry.

requirements.requirement: Submit licence renewal application to the Chief Executive not less than 30 days before the licence expiry date
requirements.rationale: Late renewal application may result in lapse of licence, making continued service provision unlawful under s28

requirements.requirement: Comply with all requirements of s28 for licence renewal as they apply to renewal applications
requirements.rationale: Renewal is subject to the same requirements as initial grant; non-compliance may result in renewal refusal

technical.summary: Technical controls to support licence renewal compliance.

requirements.requirement: Implement automated reminders for licence expiry at 90, 60, and 30 days before expiry to ensure timely renewal application
requirements.rationale: Automated reminders prevent inadvertent licence lapse due to administrative oversight

governance.summary: Governance for licence lifecycle management.

requirements.requirement: Maintain a licence lifecycle register tracking grant date, expiry date, renewal deadlines, and renewal application status
requirements.rationale: Structured licence tracking prevents lapse and provides evidence of compliance for auditors

legal.summary: The Chief Executive may suspend or revoke a licence for contravention, fraud, cessation of business, or public interest reasons.

requirements.requirement: Maintain continuous compliance with all Act 854 provisions and licence conditions to avoid suspension or revocation grounds under s30(1)(a)
requirements.rationale: Any contravention of the Act or licence conditions is a ground for suspension or revocation

requirements.requirement: Ensure all representations made in licence applications and renewals are truthful and complete, with no fraud, misrepresentation, or concealment of material facts
requirements.rationale: Fraud or misrepresentation in obtaining a licence is a ground for revocation under s30(1)(c)

technical.summary: Technical controls supporting ongoing licence compliance.

requirements.requirement: Implement compliance monitoring dashboards tracking adherence to all licence conditions and Act 854 obligations in near real-time
requirements.rationale: Proactive compliance monitoring prevents inadvertent contravention that could trigger suspension or revocation

governance.summary: Governance for managing suspension and revocation risk.

requirements.requirement: Establish a licence risk register identifying and mitigating risks of suspension or revocation, with escalation to the board for high-risk items
requirements.rationale: Proactive risk management prevents licence loss, which would halt all cyber security service provision

legal.summary: The Chief Executive maintains a register of licensees with prescribed particulars; register extracts are prima facie evidence.

requirements.requirement: Verify that engaged cyber security service providers hold valid licences by checking the Chief Executive's register of licensees
requirements.rationale: Engaging unlicensed providers may expose the entity to liability and compliance risk

requirements.requirement: For entities that are licensees: ensure registered particulars are current and accurate, notifying the Chief Executive of any changes
requirements.rationale: Inaccurate register particulars may constitute a licence condition breach

technical.summary: Technical controls for licensee verification.

requirements.requirement: Integrate licensee register verification into vendor onboarding and periodic review workflows
requirements.rationale: Automated verification prevents engagement of unlicensed providers

governance.summary: Governance for licensee register usage.

requirements.requirement: Include licensee register verification as a mandatory step in vendor onboarding procedures for all cyber security service providers
requirements.rationale: Procedural requirement ensures consistent verification across all procurements

legal.summary: Upon suspension or revocation, the licensee must immediately cease providing cyber security services; prior liabilities remain.

requirements.requirement: Immediately cease all cyber security service provision upon suspension or revocation of licence
requirements.rationale: Continuing to provide services after suspension or revocation constitutes an offence under s28

requirements.requirement: Continue to honour all liabilities incurred before the date of suspension or revocation
requirements.rationale: Suspension or revocation does not release the entity from pre-existing contractual or statutory obligations

technical.summary: Technical procedures for service cessation upon licence suspension/revocation.

requirements.requirement: Maintain a documented service cessation procedure that can be activated immediately upon licence suspension or revocation, including client notification, access revocation, and data handling
requirements.rationale: Orderly cessation prevents further offences and protects client data and systems

governance.summary: Governance for licence loss contingency planning.

requirements.requirement: Include licence loss as a scenario in business continuity planning, with pre-identified alternative service arrangements for clients
requirements.rationale: Licence suspension or revocation requires immediate service cessation; planning prevents chaos and additional liability

legal.summary: Authorized officers may investigate any offence under the Act.

requirements.requirement: Cooperate fully with authorized officers conducting investigations under the Act, providing access to premises, systems, documents, and personnel as required
requirements.rationale: Non-cooperation may constitute obstruction under s44, carrying penalties of RM300,000/5 years

technical.summary: Technical readiness for investigation cooperation.

requirements.requirement: Maintain forensic readiness including tamper-evident logging, evidence preservation capability, and chain of custody procedures for digital evidence
requirements.rationale: Forensic readiness supports effective investigation cooperation and protects the entity's interests

requirements.requirement: Ensure log retention periods and integrity controls are sufficient to support investigation requirements
requirements.rationale: Investigations may require access to historical logs; insufficient retention undermines cooperation capability

governance.summary: Governance for investigation readiness.

requirements.requirement: Establish an investigation cooperation protocol with designated liaison officers, legal counsel notification triggers, and staff briefing procedures
requirements.rationale: Structured response prevents accidental obstruction while protecting the entity's rights

legal.summary: Magistrate may issue warrants for search and seizure of computers, data, documents, and other materials.

requirements.requirement: Permit authorized officers executing a valid search warrant to enter premises and search for and seize computers, computer systems, data, documents, and other materials
requirements.rationale: Obstruction of warrant execution constitutes an offence under s44

technical.summary: Technical readiness for warrant-based search cooperation.

requirements.requirement: Be prepared to provide technical access to computer systems, networks, and data storage during warrant execution, including passwords, decryption keys, and access credentials
requirements.rationale: Technical access is required for effective warrant execution; refusal constitutes obstruction

governance.summary: Governance for search warrant response.

requirements.requirement: Establish a documented search warrant response procedure covering warrant verification, designated escort, legal counsel notification, and documentation of seized items
requirements.rationale: Structured response ensures cooperation while protecting the entity's rights and recording seized assets

legal.summary: Authorized officers may enter premises and exercise search and seizure powers without a warrant in urgent circumstances.

requirements.requirement: Permit authorized officers to enter premises without a warrant when they have reasonable cause to believe delay would adversely affect the investigation, or evidence would be tampered with
requirements.rationale: Warrantless entry is lawful under s36; refusing entry constitutes obstruction under s44

technical.summary: Technical readiness for warrantless search cooperation.

requirements.requirement: Ensure the same technical cooperation capabilities are available for warrantless searches as for warrant-based searches, including immediate system access and data production
requirements.rationale: The urgency of warrantless searches makes delayed technical cooperation more likely to constitute obstruction

governance.summary: Governance for warrantless search scenarios.

requirements.requirement: Ensure the search response procedure distinguishes between warrant-based and warrantless searches, with staff trained that both require full cooperation
requirements.rationale: Staff may incorrectly refuse entry without a warrant; training prevents inadvertent obstruction

legal.summary: Authorized officers may access computerized data and require provision of passwords, encryption keys, and decryption assistance.

requirements.requirement: Provide authorized officers with passwords, encryption keys, decryption codes, and any other means required to access computerized data during investigations
requirements.rationale: Refusal to provide access constitutes obstruction under s44; penalty RM300,000/5 years

technical.summary: Technical capability to provide computerized data access.

requirements.requirement: Maintain a secure process for emergency disclosure of passwords, encryption keys, and decryption codes to authorized officers, including break-glass procedures for key management systems
requirements.rationale: Technical inability to provide access does not excuse non-compliance; break-glass procedures ensure capability

requirements.requirement: Ensure encryption key management systems allow authorized emergency access and that key escrow or recovery mechanisms are in place for NCII systems
requirements.rationale: Lost or irrecoverable encryption keys would make compliance with s37 impossible

governance.summary: Governance for computerized data access during enforcement.

requirements.requirement: Document a clear chain of authority for authorizing emergency disclosure of credentials and encryption keys, with legal counsel review and audit trail
requirements.rationale: Emergency credential disclosure requires governance controls to prevent unauthorized use while ensuring compliance

legal.summary: The Chief Executive or authorized officers may intercept communications relevant to an investigation under the Act.

requirements.requirement: Cooperate with lawful interception orders from the Chief Executive or authorized officers, providing access to communication systems as required
requirements.rationale: Communication interception under s38 overrides other written laws; non-cooperation constitutes obstruction

technical.summary: Technical capability to support lawful interception.

requirements.requirement: Assess and document the entity's technical capability to facilitate lawful interception of communications across all communication platforms, including email, messaging, VoIP, and cloud services
requirements.rationale: Technical capability assessment identifies gaps that must be addressed before an interception order is received

governance.summary: Governance for lawful interception compliance.

requirements.requirement: Establish a lawful interception cooperation procedure that includes legal verification of the interception authorization, technical facilitation, and confidentiality requirements
requirements.rationale: Structured procedure ensures compliance while maintaining confidentiality of interception activities

legal.summary: Authorized officers must prepare a signed list of all seized items; a copy is provided to the occupier.

requirements.requirement: Ensure a designated representative is available to review, verify, and countersign the list of seized items prepared by the authorized officer
requirements.rationale: The occupier's signature confirms the accuracy of the seizure list; failure to verify may result in disputes

technical.summary: Technical support for seizure documentation.

requirements.requirement: Maintain an accessible and current asset register that can be cross-referenced against seizure lists to verify the identity and classification of seized items
requirements.rationale: Asset register cross-referencing ensures seized items are correctly identified, particularly for NCII-classified assets

governance.summary: Governance for seizure list management.

requirements.requirement: Retain copies of all seizure lists in compliance records with cross-references to the entity's asset register and any related proceedings
requirements.rationale: Seizure list copies are essential evidence for subsequent proceedings, release applications, and insurance claims

s40.title: Power to require attendance of persons

legal.summary: Authorized officers may require any person acquainted with the facts to attend and answer questions; failure to attend without reasonable excuse is an offence.

requirements.requirement: Ensure staff comply with written notices requiring attendance before authorized officers, attending as required and answering questions truthfully
requirements.rationale: Failure to attend without reasonable excuse is a criminal offence under s40(3)

technical.summary: No specific technical requirements for this section.

governance.summary: Governance for staff attendance at enforcement proceedings.

requirements.requirement: Establish a procedure for responding to attendance notices, including legal counsel notification, staff preparation, and record-keeping of notices received and attendance provided
requirements.rationale: Structured response ensures staff attend with appropriate preparation and legal support

legal.summary: Authorized officers may orally examine persons acquainted with case facts; persons are legally bound to answer truthfully and statements may be used as evidence.

requirements.requirement: Staff under examination must answer all questions relating to the case truthfully; statements will be reduced to writing, signed, and may be used as evidence
requirements.rationale: Persons are legally bound to answer under s41(2); false statements may constitute obstruction or a separate offence

technical.summary: No specific technical requirements for this section.

governance.summary: Governance for staff examination by authorized officers.

requirements.requirement: Provide legal support to staff being examined, including legal briefing on rights and obligations, and obtain copies of signed statements for entity records
requirements.rationale: Legal support protects both the staff member and the entity; statement copies are essential for defence preparation

legal.summary: Statements made to authorized officers during investigations are admissible in evidence, except statements made under arrest.

requirements.requirement: Ensure staff and legal counsel understand that statements made during investigations (not under arrest) are admissible as evidence in court proceedings
requirements.rationale: Awareness of admissibility ensures staff give careful, accurate statements and understand their evidentiary weight

technical.summary: No specific technical requirements for this section.

governance.summary: Governance for managing statement admissibility awareness.

requirements.requirement: Include statement admissibility rules in investigation readiness training, emphasizing the distinction between voluntary and custodial statements
requirements.rationale: Understanding the admissibility distinction helps staff and legal counsel prepare appropriate responses

legal.summary: Authorized officers may arrest without warrant any person reasonably believed to have committed an offence under the Act; arrested persons must be brought before a Magistrate without unnecessary delay.

requirements.requirement: Do not obstruct the arrest of any person by an authorized officer exercising powers under s43
requirements.rationale: Obstruction of arrest constitutes an offence under s44

technical.summary: No specific technical requirements for this section.

governance.summary: Governance for arrest scenarios.

requirements.requirement: Establish an arrest response protocol covering legal representation, HR notification, management escalation, and business continuity for affected functions
requirements.rationale: Structured response ensures the entity meets its duty of care to the staff member while maintaining operations

legal.summary: Authorized officers may seize movable property suspected to be evidence of or connected with an offence.

requirements.requirement: Cooperate with seizure of movable property by authorized officers and ensure a list of seized property is prepared and a copy delivered to the entity
requirements.rationale: Non-cooperation constitutes obstruction; proper documentation protects both parties

technical.summary: Technical readiness for property seizure cooperation.

requirements.requirement: Maintain up-to-date asset registers that can quickly identify seized equipment, including serial numbers, NCII classification, and data contents, to support seizure documentation and business continuity
requirements.rationale: Rapid identification of seized assets enables accurate documentation and activation of contingency plans

governance.summary: Governance for property seizure scenarios.

requirements.requirement: Include critical equipment seizure as a business continuity scenario with pre-identified alternative arrangements and recovery procedures
requirements.rationale: Seizure of critical NCII equipment may disrupt essential services; contingency planning ensures continuity

legal.summary: Seized property is liable to forfeiture but may be returned if not required for proceedings; aggrieved persons may apply to court.

requirements.requirement: Apply to the Chief Executive for release of seized property when it is no longer required for proceedings, or to the court if the Chief Executive refuses release
requirements.rationale: Timely release applications recover seized assets and reduce operational disruption

technical.summary: No specific technical requirements for this section.

governance.summary: Governance for seized property release management.

requirements.requirement: Track all seized property with regular status reviews and proactive release applications, including security verification procedures before re-integrating returned property into NCII systems
requirements.rationale: Returned property may have been compromised during custody; security verification before re-integration protects NCII systems

legal.summary: Custody costs for seized property are a charge on the property and recoverable from the convicted person.

requirements.requirement: Be aware that custody costs for seized property may be charged against the property and recovered from the entity upon conviction
requirements.rationale: Custody costs represent an additional financial liability upon conviction that should be factored into risk assessments and legal strategy

technical.summary: No specific technical requirements for this section.

governance.summary: Governance for custody cost provisioning.

requirements.requirement: Include potential custody costs as a line item in enforcement risk assessments and ensure financial provisioning or insurance coverage is assessed
requirements.rationale: Unbudgeted custody costs upon conviction represent an additional financial exposure that should be managed

legal.summary: Persons aggrieved by Chief Executive decisions may appeal to the Minister within 30 days; the Minister's decision is final.

requirements.requirement: File any appeal to the Minister within 30 days of being notified of the Chief Executive's decision
requirements.rationale: The 30-day appeal window is a strict deadline; failure to appeal within the period extinguishes the right of appeal

requirements.requirement: Accept that the Minister's decision on appeal is final and not subject to further administrative appeal
requirements.rationale: Understanding finality provisions enables proper legal strategy, including consideration of judicial review as an alternative remedy

technical.summary: No specific technical requirements for this section.

governance.summary: Governance for managing appeals.

requirements.requirement: Establish an appeal management procedure including deadline tracking, legal counsel engagement, evidence compilation, and board notification for material appeals
requirements.rationale: Structured appeal management prevents deadline misses and ensures the strongest case is presented

legal.summary: Informer identity and information are protected; no witness may be compelled to disclose informer identity except where the court determines it is necessary for justice.

requirements.requirement: Protect the identity of any person who reports cyber security offences or provides information to NACSA, in alignment with s50 informer protections
requirements.rationale: Informer protection encourages reporting and is a statutory obligation; breaching protection may deter future reporting

technical.summary: Technical controls for informer protection.

requirements.requirement: Implement anonymous or confidential reporting channels with technical controls that protect informer identity, including encrypted communication and restricted access to report details
requirements.rationale: Technical identity protection makes the statutory protection effective in practice

governance.summary: Governance for whistleblower and informer protection.

requirements.requirement: Establish a whistleblower protection policy referencing Act 854 s50 provisions, with anti-retaliation measures and regular staff awareness activities
requirements.rationale: Formal policy and awareness promote a culture of reporting while protecting informers

legal.summary: Agent provocateur evidence is admissible and the agent is not presumed unworthy of credit merely for involvement in the offence.

requirements.requirement: Ensure the legal team understands that s51 modifies common law rules on agent provocateur credibility, making such evidence admissible without the usual credibility presumption
requirements.rationale: Defence strategy must account for the enhanced admissibility of agent provocateur evidence under Act 854

technical.summary: No specific technical requirements for this section.

governance.summary: Governance for legal preparedness.

requirements.requirement: Include Act 854 evidence provisions (s41, s42, s51) in the legal team's knowledge base and continuing education programme
requirements.rationale: Awareness of non-standard evidence rules prevents tactical legal errors in enforcement proceedings

legal.summary: No prosecution under the Act may be instituted without the written consent of the Public Prosecutor.

requirements.requirement: Understand that prosecution under Act 854 requires the Public Prosecutor's written consent, which may affect pre-trial negotiation strategy
requirements.rationale: The Public Prosecutor's consent requirement provides a checkpoint that may be relevant to defence strategy and compounding negotiations

technical.summary: No specific technical requirements for this section.

governance.summary: Governance for prosecution response.

requirements.requirement: Establish a prosecution response protocol including immediate legal counsel engagement, evidence preservation, board notification, and communication strategy
requirements.rationale: Structured prosecution response prevents evidence spoliation and ensures coordinated defence

legal.summary: The Chief Executive may compound prescribed offences with Public Prosecutor consent; compound amount shall not exceed 50% of the maximum fine.

requirements.requirement: Understand compounding options and that compound amounts cannot exceed 50% of the maximum fine for the offence
requirements.rationale: Compounding offers an alternative to prosecution that may be more cost-effective and less disruptive than trial

requirements.requirement: Accept that payment of compound releases the entity from further proceedings for the compounded offence
requirements.rationale: Understanding the finality of compounding helps in decision-making between compounding and contesting prosecution

technical.summary: No specific technical requirements for this section.

governance.summary: Governance for compounding decisions.

requirements.requirement: Establish a decision framework for evaluating compounding offers versus contesting prosecution, with board approval for material amounts
requirements.rationale: Compounding decisions involve significant financial and reputational implications requiring governance oversight

s55.title: Protection against suits and legal proceedings

legal.summary: No action or suit may be brought against the CE, authorized officers, or persons acting in good faith under the Act.

requirements.requirement: Understand that the Chief Executive, authorized officers, and persons acting under the Act in good faith have statutory immunity from suits
requirements.rationale: Statutory immunity limits the entity's legal remedies against regulatory actions; alternative remedies such as s48 appeal must be pursued

requirements.requirement: Document compliance with directives and orders in good faith to benefit from the protection under s55(b) for persons acting in compliance
requirements.rationale: Documented good faith compliance may provide protection if the entity faces suit for actions taken in compliance with regulatory directives

technical.summary: No specific technical requirements for this section.

governance.summary: Governance for managing statutory immunity implications.

requirements.requirement: Maintain an audit trail of compliance decisions and good faith efforts, documenting rationale, legal advice obtained, and actions taken under regulatory direction
requirements.rationale: Audit trail of good faith compliance supports both the entity's protection under s55(b) and its position in any subsequent proceedings

legal.summary: Pre-existing NCII operators must register with the Chief Executive within the prescribed period and are deemed NCII entities until formally designated.

requirements.requirement: If operating or managing NCII before commencement, register with the Chief Executive within the prescribed period
requirements.rationale: Failure to register within the prescribed period may result in enforcement action for non-compliance

requirements.requirement: Comply with all NCII entity duties under the Act during the deemed status period, pending formal designation under s17
requirements.rationale: Deemed entities have the same obligations as formally designated entities; non-compliance during transition is not excused

technical.summary: Technical readiness during transition period.

requirements.requirement: Implement all technical security controls required of NCII entities during the deemed status period, not waiting for formal designation
requirements.rationale: Deemed entities must meet the same technical standards; delaying implementation creates compliance gaps

governance.summary: Governance for managing transitional NCII entity status.

requirements.requirement: Develop a transition plan with milestones for achieving full NCII entity compliance, including registration, gap assessment, and remediation
requirements.rationale: Structured transition planning ensures compliance obligations are met within prescribed periods

s58.title: Transitional provision for cyber security service providers

legal.summary: Pre-existing cyber security service providers must apply for a licence within the prescribed period and hold deemed licence status until the application is determined.

requirements.requirement: If providing cyber security services before commencement, apply for a licence under s28 within the prescribed period
requirements.rationale: Failure to apply within the prescribed period may result in loss of deemed licence status and unlawful service provision

requirements.requirement: Comply with all licensee obligations during the deemed licence period while the application is being determined
requirements.rationale: Deemed licence holders have the same obligations as fully licensed providers

technical.summary: No specific technical requirements beyond s28 and s32 obligations.

governance.summary: Governance for managing transitional licensing.

requirements.requirement: Track the licence application status and maintain evidence of deemed licence compliance throughout the determination period
requirements.rationale: Evidence of compliance during the deemed licence period protects against enforcement action if determination is delayed

legal.summary: Pre-commencement investigations and proceedings continue under the prior written law as if Act 854 had not been enacted.

requirements.requirement: Identify any pre-commencement investigations or proceedings and ensure they are being managed under the correct prior legal framework, not under Act 854
requirements.rationale: Applying Act 854 retrospectively to pre-commencement proceedings would be legally incorrect under s59

technical.summary: No specific technical requirements for this section.

governance.summary: Governance for managing transitional proceedings.

requirements.requirement: Maintain a register distinguishing between pre-commencement and post-commencement proceedings, with the applicable legal framework identified for each
requirements.rationale: Clear distinction prevents legal errors in managing ongoing proceedings during the transition period

legal.summary: The Minister may make regulations for risk assessments, audits, licensing, incident notification, compounding, and other Act 854 purposes.

requirements.requirement: Monitor the Gazette for new regulations made under s60 and implement compliance requirements within prescribed timeframes
requirements.rationale: Regulations carry the force of law; non-compliance with regulations is equivalent to non-compliance with the Act

requirements.requirement: Comply with all existing regulations (P.U.(A) 219-222/2024) covering risk assessment, incident notification, licensing, and compounding
requirements.rationale: These four regulations are in force and create binding compliance obligations

technical.summary: Technical compliance with subsidiary regulations.

requirements.requirement: Implement technical controls required by subsidiary regulations, including automated incident notification (P.U.(A) 220), risk assessment tooling (P.U.(A) 219), and licensing compliance systems (P.U.(A) 221)
requirements.rationale: Subsidiary regulations prescribe specific technical requirements that supplement the Act's general obligations

governance.summary: Governance for regulatory monitoring and implementation.

requirements.requirement: Establish a regulatory monitoring process that systematically identifies new regulations under s60, assesses applicability, and tracks implementation to completion
requirements.rationale: Proactive regulatory monitoring prevents compliance gaps arising from undetected new regulations
